Ji Mr Anthony, main aapko isliye call kar rahi hoon kyunki aapke account mein kuch invoices overdue hain, total INR57,920. Language coach: The customer is speaking in English. Reply entirely in English on your very next turn. Use zero Hindi, Hinglish, Bengali, or mixed-language filler.

Sure, Mr Anthony. You have three overdue invoices: DHL123456 for INR13,600 due on January31, DHL654321 for INR34,650 due on February22, and DHL332241 for INR9,670 due on April7. All disputes are resolved, and credit notes issued, so only payment is pending.
Disputes on these invoices?

Certainly, Mr Anthony. For invoice DHL123456, you had raised a price-list versus invoice mismatch on one waybill, which was resolved and a credit note was issued. For DHL654321, you logged an issue for two delayed shipments; this was also resolved with a credit note issued, and you confirmed receipt of it. There are no disputes on invoice DHL332241. All issues are closed, and only payment is pending.
I can only pay for one.
I understand, Mr Anthony. Could you please let me know which invoice you will be able to pay, and by when? Remember, the payment date should be within the next two business days.
I would have to talk to my boss.

I am not the right person to follow up with.
I understand, Mr Anthony. Could you please connect me with Mrs Anna Gressive or the right person available on the line now so we can discuss the payment?
Before that, tell me how I can pay.

You can pay using the DHL MyBill self-serve portal by logging in at mybill.dhl.com with your registered email and password, where you can select and pay your invoices online. Alternatively, you can transfer the exact invoice amount to your unique Virtual Account Number via NEFT or RTGS bank transfer. Let me know if you need the Virtual Account Number details.
Okay, fine, I'll pay you tomorrow.
Thank you, Mr Anthony. I have noted your promise to pay by tomorrow. Please ensure the payment is made within the next two business days. If you need any assistance, feel free to contact us.
